name: Butterflied Leg of Lamb with Roasted Vegetabl
recipeIngredient: 6 lb Lamb, leg of, boned and butterflied
5 large Potatoes, baking cut into 6 lengthwise wedges each
4 Red Bell Peppers, seeded, halved lengthwise
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 2/3 cup of the olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, 2 tablespoons of marjoram, and rosemary. Marinate the lamb in this mixture for 24 hours.
2. Bring the lamb to room temperature.
3. Preheat broiler and broiler pan. Remove lamb from marinade, scraping off as much herbs and garlic as possible. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Place lamb flat on the broiler pan, thickest part at the back. Broil for 15 minutes.
5. Turn lamb and continue broiling for 13 to 15 more minutes. Let stand 10 minutes.
6. Combine remaining potatoes, vegetables, and olive oil in a large roasting pan. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Roast in oven until potatoes are tender when pierced with a knife and vegetables are just tender, about 25 to 30 minutes.
8. Reserve 10 potato wedges for later use. Serve remaining roasted vegetables with sliced lamb and lemon wedges.
Note: This cut is also excellent for grilling. Grill until slight pink remains in the center over low direct heat or smoke-roast over indirect heat. The reserved potatoes can be used for hash later in the week. Reheat vegetables during the 10 minutes lamb stands before carving.
